turns.content: Hey! I'm trying to build a shell-like interface for my project using the rustyline crate, but I think I just misunderstood how history works. I thought once you set up the editor state with EditState and register the callbacks, the whole command history would automatically get saved to disk without needing any extra config options.

That's a great intuition but it has a small catch. The history is actually stored in an internal History object which you instantiate with History::new() or similar, and you must explicitly enable persistence via the rustyline::history::History::load_from_file() method if you want to save commands back to a file on startup.

Oh I see now, so my code was missing that load call. But wait, does this mean tab completion for syntax highlighting is also just disabled by default? My rustyline instance isn't showing any colored completions when I type a function name.

You're right to check that; the built-in completion only returns raw strings unless you register custom handlers. To get syntax highlighting you need to implement a custom Completer trait or use rustyline's suggest feature which requires setting up a Suggester object before initializing your editor state.
